title: Cellulitis follow-up documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, lack of improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, spread, underlying conditions, serious alternative diagnoses, and microbiology results during clinician reassessment.
assessment.rationale: NG141 directly supports documenting interval change, spread, severe or disproportionate pain, systemic illness, underlying predisposition, previous antimicrobial use, and microbiology during cellulitis reassessment without deciding treatment.

source.title: Cellulitis and erysipelas: antimicrobial prescribing
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: Adults, young people, and children with cellulitis or erysipelas.
source.setting: Initial and follow-up assessment, antimicrobial review, reassessment, and referral decisions.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting cellulitis extent, site, severity, exposure and microbiology context, reassessment, worsening, severe pain, systemic illness, and clinician decisions. It does not diagnose infection, prescribe antibiotics, or generate referral.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting rapid or significant worsening, lack of improvement, systemic illness, pain out of proportion, spread, underlying conditions, serious alternative diagnoses, and microbiology results during clinician reassessment.

assessment.text: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.
assessment.rationale: The referral section supports recording a clinician-made escalation or specialist decision and follow-up discussion, not generating one.

source.title: Cellulitis and erysipelas: antimicrobial prescribing
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.13–1.1.14 — clinician referral and specialist advice criteria

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting clinician-identified serious illness, periorbital location, unusual exposure, treatment non-response, lymphangitis, oral-intake limitation, and an already made referral or specialist discussion; it does not generate urgency.

assessment.text: Supports documenting skin redness and swelling extent, site, severity, broken skin, penetrating or water exposure, microbiology already obtained, skin-tone visibility, and treatment already prescribed without generating diagnosis or treatment.
assessment.rationale: Initial-site, extent, exposure, and prior-treatment context was reviewed for follow-up applicability.
